title: Document Sharing UI Redesign
summary: Replace the temporary document-package route composition with index-owned preparation and a scope-owned package inbox.
content_html:
Document Sharing UI Redesign

Purpose

Replace the former route-shaped document-sharing UI now that Docs Viewer has a canonical multiple-selection interaction. Prepare no longer duplicates selection in a dedicated page; Returned Packages stays separate because it is a scope-owned whole-package workflow rather than a selection consumer.

Proposed Direction

  • Checked documents in the Docs Viewer index become the canonical input to Prepare package.
  • The Actions command opens a compact modal for profile, format, descendant, and confirmation choices; it does not reproduce the document tree or scope selection.
  • Returned packages opens a scope-owned package inbox hosted by Docs Viewer.
  • Returned packages is not a selection consumer. A returned package remains one whole, atomic, read-only record set. Its rows do not participate in index selection, filtering, or partial apply.
  • Existing package services, trusted metadata, scope partitioning, validation, review, and canonical handoff contracts remain in place.

Delivery

Prepare Package UI Redesign Delivery is complete. The shipped Prepare Package user workflow records its selection rules and action calls. Returned Packages remains a separate later slice and keeps this parent in progress.

Transition

The checkbox-selection dependency and the first Prepare package consumer are shipped. Its compact Action workflow owns the operation, and the duplicate Prepare route is retired without an alias. Keep the Returned Packages route unchanged and make only necessary correctness, accessibility, and maintenance fixes there.

The Prepare delivery retired only its route after the selection Action worked end to end. The Returned Packages route remains operational until its own later delivery. Neither cutover adds compatibility aliases.

Related Feature

Share Document Packages remains the shipped authority for document-package behavior and service ownership. This feature changes only its browser composition.
